The plant Pisum sativum, commonly known as the garden pea, is a cool-season legume that belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is rich in health benefits, including being a good source of protein, fiber, and essential nutrients like vitamin K and folate, which can help lower cholesterol levels and support heart health. The bioactive constituents of Pisum sativum include isoflavones, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiproliferative effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Pisum sativum include freeze-dried pea powder, tea, and capsules, which are used to support cardiovascular health, digestive function, and immune system function.
